understanding.goal_understanding: Progress through each KA59 level by arranging all selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked '+' is occupied at the same time; a level is only cleared when all goals are simultaneously covered, and clearing all levels wins the run within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The currently selected piece (shown as 'P' in the semantic grid) moves in 3-pixel grid increments via MOVE actions; movement can be blocked or altered by walls/boundaries, with movement.*_blocked serving only as an adjacency hint rather than a guaranteed failure. Pushable blocks ('B') can be moved when adjacent in the direction of movement, shifting along with the selected piece if space permits, while walls ('#') prevent movement/pushing depending on their specific collision behavior. When multiple selectables exist ('S' for non-selected), control switches using CLICK on that piece’s exact pixel position as listed in the state/objects, after which MOVE actions apply to the newly selected piece.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Progress through each KA59 level by arranging all selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked '+' is occupied at the same time; a level is only cleared when all goals are simultaneously covered, and clearing all levels wins the run within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The currently selected piece (shown as 'P' in the semantic grid) moves in 3-pixel grid increments via MOVE actions; movement can be blocked or altered by walls/boundaries, with movement.*_blocked serving only as an adjacency hint rather than a guaranteed failure. Pushable blocks ('B') can be moved when adjacent in the direction of movement, shifting along with the selected piece if space permits, while walls ('#') prevent movement/pushing depending on their specific collision behavior. When multiple selectables exist ('S' for non-selected), control switches using CLICK on that piece’s exact pixel position as listed in the state/objects, after which MOVE actions apply to the newly selected piece.
